herb_parts.name: root
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica root is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The root is believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever and treating skin conditions. In some traditional remedies, the root is used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.

herb_parts.name: leaf
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica leaf is known for its medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The leaf extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, skin infections, and digestive issues. Its leaf also contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties.

herb_parts.name: rhizome
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica rhizome is primarily used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate pain and reduce swelling in various health conditions. The rhizome is also valued for its antimicrobial properties, which aid in fighting off infections and promoting wound healing. In addition, it is believed to possess antioxidant properties, which help to protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage.

herb_parts.name: radical leaf
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica radical leaf is a medicinal part of the neem tree that is used to treat various health issues. The radical leaf is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and other inflammatory conditions. It is also used in traditional medicine to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal disorders.

herb_parts.name: stem
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica stem, also known as neem stem bark, is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The stem bark is a rich source of limonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. It is also used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its antiseptic and astringent properties.

herb_parts.name: inflorescence
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica inflorescence, which is the flowering part of the neem tree, contains various bioactive compounds that have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The inflorescence of Azadirachta indica has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory disorders. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of neem oil and other bioactive compounds that have been isolated from the inflorescence.

herb_parts.name: cauline leaf
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica cauline leaf is a medicinal part known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various skin infections and wounds. The leaf is also rich in antioxidants, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat fever, boils, and other skin-related conditions.

herb_parts.name: flower
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica flower is used in traditional medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The flowers are believed to have a soothing effect on the skin, making them useful in treating various skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Additionally, the flower is also used to treat fever and respiratory issues due to its antipyretic and expectorant properties.

herb_parts.name: petiole
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica petiole, also known as the leaf stalk, contains flavonoids and terpenoids which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The petiole is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems. Its medicinal properties also contribute to its potential in managing inflammatory conditions and promoting overall well-being.

herb_parts.name: stamen
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica stamen, also known as the flower of neem, is a rich source of azadirachtin, a biopesticide that helps in controlling various insect pests and diseases. The stamen of Azadirachta indica contains flavonoids, triterpenoids, and other compounds with medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The extracts from the stamen have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its medicinal value.

herb_parts.name: seeds
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica seeds contain nimbin and nimbidin, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The seeds are also a rich source of azadirachtin, a compound with insecticidal and antifeedant properties that can help in controlling insect pests. Additionally, the seeds contain nimolicine, which has been shown to have antimalarial and antifungal properties.

herb_parts.name: floral bud
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica floral bud is rich in nimbin and nimbidin, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The floral bud of Azadirachta indica is also used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant properties. Its bioactive compounds, such as azadirachtin and 6-desacetyllimocitine, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.

herb_parts.name: pistil
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica pistil is a rich source of azadirachtin, a potent bioactive compound with insecticidal properties. The pistil of the neem tree is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. Additionally, the pistil has been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, making it a valuable component in Ayurvedic and Unani medicinal practices.

herb_parts.name: fruit
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica fruit, commonly known as neem fruit, is rich in nutrients and antioxidants, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The fruit is a good source of vitamins A and C, as well as minerals like potassium and iron, making it beneficial for overall health. Additionally, the fruit has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and fight off infections.

herb_parts.name: sepal
herb_parts.desc: N/A

herb_parts.name: petal
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica petal is not commonly used in traditional medicine, but some studies suggest it may have antimicrobial properties. However, there is limited research on the medicinal properties of the Azadirachta indica petal, and more studies are needed to confirm its potential uses. In general, the more commonly used medicinal parts of Azadirachta indica include the leaves, bark, and seeds.

herb_parts.name: style
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica style is a part of the neem tree that has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. The leaves, bark, roots, and seeds of the neem tree are used for medicinal purposes, including reducing fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The fruit, seeds, and leaves of the neem tree are also used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and wounds.

herb_parts.name: stigma
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica stigma is a lesser-known part of the neem tree that has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. The stigma is rich in bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These properties make the Azadirachta indica stigma a potential natural remedy for various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues.

herb_parts.name: bark
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica bark contains a compound called nimbin, which has been traditionally used to treat fever and rheumatism, and is also known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bark is also a source of tannins and flavonoids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. However, further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Azadirachta indica bark.

herb_parts.name: nectar
herb_parts.desc: Azadirachta indica nectar, although not a primary focus of traditional medicine, contains triterpenoids and flavonoids that have been reported to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. These compounds may contribute to the plant's overall health benefits when consumed or applied topically. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Azadirachta indica nectar.
